text:
Identity on the internet has evolved in silos:
 • 1Password (for Zero-Knowledge Privacy)
 • Google (for OAuth Convenience)
 • DigiLocker (for Government Verification)
 • Stripe (for Developer Experience)

But all of them missed the biggest crisis of the AI era: 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗕𝗼𝘁𝘀 and 𝗙𝗮𝗸𝗲 𝗔𝗰𝗰𝗼𝘂𝗻𝘁𝘀.

You can store a password securely, and you can log in quickly, but neither proves you are a real human.

With Sva, we are merging these four philosophies into a single protocol:

 • From the 𝗨𝗫 world: We took the OAuth 2.0 standard. (𝘖𝘯𝘦-𝘤𝘭𝘪𝘤𝘬 𝘭𝘰𝘨𝘪𝘯 𝘴𝘪𝘨𝘶𝘱).
 • From the 𝗗𝗲𝘃 world: We built it API-first. (𝘋𝘳𝘰𝘱-𝘪𝘯 𝘪𝘯𝘵𝘦𝘨𝘳𝘢𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯 𝘭𝘪𝘬𝘦 𝘚𝘵𝘳𝘪𝘱𝘦).
 • From the 𝗣𝗿𝗶𝘃𝗮𝗰𝘆 world: We took the Zero-Knowledge Vault. (𝘞𝘦 𝘤𝘢𝘯'𝘵 𝘴𝘦𝘦 𝘺𝘰𝘶𝘳 𝘥𝘢𝘵𝘢).
 • From the 𝗧𝗿𝘂𝘀𝘁 world: We integrated Government Verification. (𝘙𝘦𝘢𝘭-𝘸𝘰𝘳𝘭𝘥 𝘱𝘳𝘰𝘰𝘧 𝘷𝘪𝘢 𝘋𝘪𝘨𝘪𝘓𝘰𝘤𝘬𝘦𝘳).

The Sva Innovation? Proof of Personhood.
By linking your digital identity to a verified government ID (𝘸𝘪𝘵𝘩𝘰𝘶𝘵 𝘴𝘵𝘰𝘳𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘪𝘵), Sva acts as a firewall for the internet.

✅ Verified Humans pass through instantly.
❌ AI Bots and Fraud Farms hit a brick wall.

We aren't just managing credentials anymore. We are verifying humanity.

#Sva #Identity #ZeroKnowledge #CyberSecurity #Bots #AI #Startup #BuildInPublic

text:
We are fast approaching the age of the "𝘿𝙚𝙖𝙙 𝙄𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙧𝙣𝙚𝙩."
𝗔𝗜 𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀, 𝗱𝗲𝗲𝗽𝗳𝗮𝗸𝗲𝘀, and sophisticated 𝗯𝗼𝘁𝘀 are making it impossible to know if the "𝙥𝙚𝙧𝙨𝙤𝙣" you are interacting with is real.

The current defenses aren't working:
❌ CAPTCHAs are failing (AI is too smart now).
❌ Uploading your Government ID to every random site is a privacy nightmare.

We are stuck between "𝗕𝗼𝘁 𝗖𝗵𝗮𝗼𝘀" and "𝗧𝗼𝘁𝗮𝗹 𝗦𝘂𝗿𝘃𝗲𝗶𝗹𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲."
I believe we need a third option: Proof of Personhood without the data trade-off.

What are your thoughts? Is there a way to solve bots without giving up our privacy?

#AI #DeadInternet #CyberSecurity #Identity #TechTrends

text:
𝗟𝗮𝘀𝘁 𝘄𝗲𝗲𝗸, I announced Sva and my mission to fix the 𝘪𝘯𝘵𝘦𝘳𝘯𝘦𝘵’𝘴 𝘣𝘳𝘰𝘬𝘦𝘯 𝘵𝘳𝘶𝘴𝘵 𝘭𝘢𝘺𝘦𝘳. Since then, I’ve been analyzing the first batch of responses from my market research survey.

The early data around 𝟭𝟯𝟬 𝗿𝗲𝘀𝗽𝗼𝗻𝘀𝗲𝘀 highlights a massive contradiction in how we live online:

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗕𝗲𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗶𝗼𝗿: Almost 100% of respondents rely on "Continue with Google" or social logins. We crave speed.

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗘𝗺𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: When asked about data breaches, the responses swing wildly between "Anxious" (changing passwords immediately) and "Resigned" (assuming leaks are inevitable).

𝗧𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗳𝗶𝗿𝗺𝘀 𝗺𝘆 𝗰𝗼𝗿𝗲 𝗵𝘆𝗽𝗼𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘀𝗶𝘀: We aren't giving up our data because we don't care. We're doing it because we feel like we have no other choice.

𝙏𝙝𝙚 𝙘𝙪𝙧𝙧𝙚𝙣𝙩 𝙞𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙧𝙣𝙚𝙩 𝙛𝙤𝙧𝙘𝙚𝙨 𝙮𝙤𝙪 𝙩𝙤 𝙘𝙝𝙤𝙤𝙨𝙚: 𝘾𝙤𝙣𝙫𝙚𝙣𝙞𝙚𝙣𝙘𝙚 𝙊𝙍 𝘾𝙤𝙣𝙩𝙧𝙤𝙡.

With 𝗦𝘃𝗮, I’m building a protocol where you get both. A one-click login (convenience) backed by a Zero-Knowledge Vault (control).

I’m still gathering data to refine the MVP. If you haven't yet, I’d love your honest take on the state of digital identity. It takes 3 minutes.

Link in comments 👇

#Sva #BuildInPublic #DataPrivacy #StartupJourney #Identity #BuildInPublic #Entrepreneurship #StartupIndia #SaaS #FounderJourney #StudentFounder #TechStartups

text:
I've spent the last few months obsessed with a single, foundational problem: the internet's trust layer is broken.

We live in a world of endless passwords, constant data breaches, and a rising tide of bots and AI-generated content that makes it impossible to know who is real.

I believe the solution requires a shift from platform-centric identity to user-sovereign identity.

That's why I'm building Sva.
It's a massive mission, and I'll be sharing my journey, my learnings, and the challenges along the way right here. Follow along if you're interested in the future of digital trust. 

#Sva #BuildInPublic #Identity #public #privacy #protection #security #developer #zeroknowledge

text:
✨ Thrilled to share a moment of pride!

Our team Electro-Coders has cleared the internal round of Smart India Hackathon 2025 successfully, earning a spot in the Top 2 teams of our college International Institute of Information Technology, Bhubaneswar. I was working on my first team leadership experience, and I'm really thankful to have shared this so far.

Our initiative, "SwachhBandhu – A Mobile-First Civic Tech Platform", is geared towards addressing day-to-day civic complaints such as trash heaps, potholes, and defunct streetlights. With offerings like QR-based reporting, geo-verification, gamification, and real-time dashboards for authorities, our vision is to create cleaner, wiser, and more responsive cities.

In spite of the stress of continuous midterm exams, the team devoted their time and effort to constructing and developing our solution. I would like to personally thank all the members of my team for their hard work, creativity, and dedication.

We have special appreciation for Anurag Singh, who presented an outstanding presentation during the internal round and exhibited our idea with clarity and confidence.

This achievement could not have happened without the coordination, determination, and mutual vision we propagated alongside. Looking forward to the subsequent phases of this venture!

#SIH2025 #SmartIndiaHackathon #Teamwork #Innovation #Leadership #CleanTech

text:
From concept to a fully deployed application, I'm incredibly proud to launch 𝐆𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐛𝐲.𝐜𝐥𝐨𝐮𝐝! 

𝐋𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐒𝐢𝐭𝐞: https://greaby.cloud

Driven by passion, I built this scalable performance optimized and accurate real-world application from the ground up, with a flexible architecture that allows effortless integration of future AI services.

𝐆𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐛𝐲.𝐜𝐥𝐨𝐮𝐝 𝐢𝐬 𝐚𝐧 𝐚𝐥𝐥-𝐢𝐧-𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐀𝐈 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 , 𝐨𝐟𝐟𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐨𝐨𝐥𝐬 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐜 𝐯𝐨𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧, 𝐡𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐥𝐲 𝐚𝐜𝐜𝐮𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐜𝐫𝐢𝐩𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧, 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐧𝐞𝐚𝐫-𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐞𝐜𝐭 𝐝𝐨𝐜𝐮𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 — 𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐰𝐡𝐢𝐥𝐞 𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐠𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐥 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐝𝐨𝐜𝐮𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭.

Building this was a fantastic technical challenge. My goal was to create a seamless user experience using the cutting edge technology and tools.

𝐁𝐚𝐜𝐤𝐞𝐧𝐝: Python, Django, Django REST Framework
𝐅𝐫𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐧𝐝: React, Vite, TanStack Query, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui
𝐀𝐬𝐲𝐧𝐜 & 𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐥-𝐓𝐢𝐦𝐞:Celery, Redis, Django Channels (WebSockets)
𝐃𝐚𝐭𝐚𝐛𝐚𝐬𝐞 & 𝐂𝐚𝐜𝐡𝐞: PostgreSQL, Azure Cache for Redis
𝐃𝐞𝐩𝐥𝐨𝐲𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭:Azure App Service (Backend), Vercel (Frontend)
𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐅𝐞𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞: Real-time WebSocket notifications for asynchronous AI tasks, providing a dynamic and responsive user experience.
𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬: Azure Cognitive Services, Razorpay API, Azure Blob Storage.

I handled everything from the initial database design to the final deployment configuration.

I would be honored if you'd take a look at the live version. All feedback is welcome!

#𝐖𝐞𝐛𝐃𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐩𝐞𝐫 #𝐂𝐥𝐨𝐮𝐝𝐀𝐫𝐜𝐡𝐢𝐭𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 #𝐏𝐲𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐧𝐃𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐩𝐞𝐫 #𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐃𝐞𝐯 #𝐃𝐣𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐨 #𝐀𝐳𝐮𝐫𝐞 #𝐏𝐨𝐫𝐭𝐟𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐨𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐣𝐞𝐜𝐭 #𝐀𝐈  #𝐒𝐨𝐟𝐭𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐄𝐧𝐠𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐞𝐫

text:
Just wrapped up an incredible two-day journey at the AMD  AI Sprint Hackathon at Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay ! 

Our team tackled Track 2: Agentic AI Scheduling Assistant, focusing on building an AI agent capable of coordinating meetings across time zones, resolving conflicts, and handling rescheduling powered by large language models and agentic workflows. It was a fascinating challenge to automate meeting coordination by scanning calendars, negotiating conflicts, and sending polite requests – aiming for a truly seamless, intelligent assistant.

A massive shoutout to my amazing teammate Rahul Samant  – couldn't have done this without you! The power of AMD's MI300 GPUs provided the computational muscle we needed for our AI processing.

Beyond the technical deep dive, it was an immense privilege to meet so many incredible people and connect with great minds like Shobhit Singh , Bhavya Pratap Singh Tomar , Janarthanan Gnanamurthy , Anupam Rawat.The insights and camaraderie made this experience truly memorable.

Huge thanks to AMD , Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay , and all the organizers for making this cutting-edge event a reality. The learnings here are invaluable!

text:
My journey in the Anveshan Startup Challenge 1.0 has been incredible with Aditya Pattanayak. While we didn't advance to the final Ofline round, we believe the mission is far more important than any single competition. The validation we received as a Top 20 finalist from Indian Institute of Management, Indore and the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has inspired us to take the next big step.

In the spirit of collaboration and public good, we are thrilled to announce that Swachh Bandhu is now a fully open-source project!

We built this platform to bridge the gap between citizens and municipalities through technology. We believe this idea is too important to keep on a shelf. Now, it belongs to the community.

What is Swachh Bandhu?
It's a complete, feature-rich civic-tech platform that enables:
📍 QR Code-Based Reporting: Citizens can instantly report sanitation issues from geo-tagged locations.
🤝 Peer-to-Peer Verification: A core feature to ensure reports are authentic, saving municipal time and resources.
🏆 Gamification & Rewards: A system of points, leaderboards, and lotteries to drive sustained citizen engagement.
📈 Powerful Admin Dashboard: Real-time analytics, heatmaps, and report management tools for city authorities.

We're Sharing Everything:
We want our work to serve as a foundation for students, developers, municipalities, and innovators who share our vision for a cleaner India. We invite you to explore, fork, build upon, or deploy our work.

Watch the Product Demo: See how Swachh Bandhu works in this short video. 
https://lnkd.in/gRTDgCdf

Dive into the Code: The complete source code, with a detailed setup guide, is available on GitHub. We built it with a modern stack (Django, React, PostgreSQL) ready for deployment.
GitHub: https://lnkd.in/guXRdJue

Understand Our Vision: View the complete pitch deck we presented during the challenge. https://lnkd.in/gmDrVn4n

Our goal was always to create a positive impact. By making Swachh Bandhu open source, we hope to empower countless others to join this mission.

A huge thank you to Indian Institute of Management, Indore, the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, and everyone who has supported our journey.

#OpenSource #SwachhBandhu #CivicTech #GovTech #MakeInIndia #DigitalIndia #SmartCities #SwachhBharat #AnveshanStartupChallenge #SocialImpact #ForPublicGood #Django #React

text:
Incredibly honored to announce that I will be representing my institution, the IIIT Bhubanswar , on the global stage!

My project, has been selected for the Grand Finale of the IEEE YESIST12 2025 - Innovation Challenge, which will be held in Malaysia this 23 & 24 of August 2025. It's a profound honor to carry the name of my university to this international event, especially after being chosen from a pool of over 1000+ global submissions.

The journey to the finale began with my project securing First Prize in the Innovation Challenge track at the preliminary round, "THE YESSIST DAY 2025," hosted by JIT IEEE & WIE STUDENT BRANCH - 09271.

I'm excited to present my project, "The AI-Powered Marketplace for Educational Simulations & Animations". Driving this initiative as a solo member has been a challenging yet immensely rewarding experience.

This achievement would not have been possible without the invaluable guidance of my mentor, Mrs. Puspanjali Mohapatra. My sincere gratitude also goes to the entire IEEE YESIST12 committee and Jansons Institute of Technology for this incredible platform.

I am eagerly looking forward to showcasing the innovation brewing at IIIT Bhubanswar and connecting with brilliant minds from around the world in Malaysia!

#IIITBhubaneswar #IIITBBSR #IEEE #YESIST12 #InnovationChallenge #GrandFinale #ProudMoment #StudentInnovation #AI #EdTech #RepresentingIndia #Malaysia
